audit_logs row exists with action='task_created' and detail.task_id matching returned task_id

INV-05: tasks.lead_id references a valid lead row (0 orphans)

test_name: TaskOverdueSweepJob marks past-due open and in_progress tasks as overdue
preconditions: Task 'task-done-002' exists: status='done', due_at=<now-3h> (must not change)

Query DB: SELECT status FROM tasks WHERE task_id='task-overdue-001'
Query DB: SELECT status FROM tasks WHERE task_id='task-overdue-002'
Query DB: SELECT status FROM tasks WHERE task_id='task-done-002'
Query DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM event_outbox WHERE event_code='TASK_OVERDUE' AND aggregate_id IN ('task-overdue-001','task-overdue-002')
expected_result: task-overdue-001 and task-overdue-002 → status='overdue'; task-done-002 unchanged; 2 TASK_OVERDUE events in event_outbox
postconditions: INV-01: no future-due task has status='overdue' (0 rows)
Sweep is idempotent — running a second time produces no new duplicate events

test_name: SLA engine computes business-hours-aware due_at skipping closing time and weekend
preconditions: BusinessCalendar: Mon–Sat 09:30–18:30 IST, Saturday is working, Sunday is holiday
test_steps: Assert result is Saturday ~12:30 IST (not Friday+4h wall-clock which would be 21:00)
Verify: 1h remaining Friday (17:00–18:00 counts; 18:00–18:30 = 30min), then Saturday 09:30 onwards for remaining ~2.5h
Assert result does NOT fall on a closed/holiday period
expected_result: computeDueAt returns 2026-06-20T12:00:00+05:30 (approx); does not return 2026-06-19T21:00:00+05:30

test_name: SLA engine resolves BusinessCalendar in branch → region → default order

test_steps: Assert branch-mumbai calendar is returned (highest priority)
Remove branch-specific calendar; call resolve again

Assert hardcoded Mon–Sat IST fallback used and warning logged
expected_result: Resolution order: branch > region > default > hardcoded-fallback; warning logged when no calendar configured

test_name: ADMIN views SLA policy list with status chips and can create new policy
preconditions: At least 2 SLA policies seeded: 1 active, 1 pending approval
test_steps: Assert DataTable renders with policy rows
Assert StatusChip shows 'Active' for active policy
Assert StatusChip shows 'Pending Approval' for pending policy
Fill form: name='E2E SLA Test', applies_to='first_contact', threshold_minutes=120
Add escalation step: at_minutes=60, notify_roles=['BM'], action='notify'
Assert new policy row appears with 'Pending Approval' chip
expected_result: Policy list renders; new policy created with 'Pending Approval' status; maker-checker confirm dialog shown
postconditions: sla_policies row with is_active=false
configuration_versions row with status='pending'

test_name: SLA policy form shows inline error for zero threshold and BM sees read-only view
test_steps: Part A (ADMIN): Navigate to /admin/sla-policies, click 'New Policy', enter threshold=0, submit
Assert inline field error under threshold input (aria-describedby set)
Assert form not submitted (network request not fired)
Part B (BM): Login as BM, navigate to /admin/sla-policies
Assert no deactivate/edit action buttons in table rows
expected_result: Part A: field error visible, form blocked. Part B: BM sees read-only list, no write actions

test_name: Transaction rollback: sla_policies and configuration_versions both absent when audit_logs insert fails
preconditions: AuditAppender mocked to throw after sla_policies INSERT and configuration_versions INSERT
No existing policy named 'Rollback Test Policy'
test_steps: AuditAppender.append() throws during the UnitOfWork transaction
Query DB (invariant): SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sla_policies WHERE name='Rollback Test Policy'
Query DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM configuration_versions WHERE config_type='sla_policy' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sla_policies sp WHERE sp.sla_policy_id = configuration_versions.config_ref)
expected_result: 0 sla_policies rows; 0 configuration_versions rows; no partial state

di_meta.setup_sql: DELETE FROM sla_policies WHERE name = 'Rollback Test Policy'; DELETE FROM configuration_versions WHERE config_type = 'sla_policy' AND config_ref NOT IN (SELECT sla_policy_id FROM sla_policies);
di_meta.action: SlaPolicyService.create() with mocked AuditAppender that throws
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sla_policies WHERE name = 'Rollback Test Policy' HAVING COUNT(*) > 0
di_meta.cleanup: DELETE FROM sla_policies WHERE name = 'Rollback Test Policy';
